authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical-record regulation. It directly supports EHR or medical-record truth-set expectations, encounter documentation, authentication, record completion, and audit traceability for case-management planning.
used_for: documentation gap and allocation blocker scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.30 - Condition of participation: Utilization review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital utilization-review regulation. It directly supports escalation when admission, continued stay, professional service, medical necessity, or level-of-care judgment is implicated.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.43 - Condition of participation: Discharge planning
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital discharge-planning regulation. It directly supports discharge planning, discharge-plan evaluation, post-hospital service evidence, transfer/referral information, patient preferences, and regular re-evaluation expectations central to hospital case management.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: discharge planning, transition, and refresh cadence
used_for: discharge target and post-acute handoff planning

authoritative_sources.title: Revisions to Hospital - Appendix A of the State Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS hospital interpretive guidance for Conditions of Participation. It is authoritative for survey, evidence, and compliance interpretation posture across hospital operational and case-management processes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: regulatory boundary and policy conflict handling
used_for: source hierarchy and regulatory conflict scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Care Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. Its guidance is authoritative for PHI use and disclosure in treatment, payment, care coordination, and health care operations contexts.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting operational PHI disclosures, role-based distribution, and privacy escalation conditions.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 1 - Inpatient Hospital Services Covered Under Part A
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS Medicare benefit manual for inpatient hospital services. It grounds inpatient, observation-adjacent, covered service, and admission terminology used in hospital case-management planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: inpatient status, benefit, and payer-boundary prerequisites

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 3 - Inpatient Hospital Billing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS claims-processing manual for inpatient hospital billing. It grounds charge, claim, adjustment, transfer, denial, and remittance artifact expectations while preserving revenue-cycle authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: charge, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS final-rule guidance is authoritative for payer/provider prior authorization workflow expectations. It supports prerequisite handling for authorization status and payer-portal evidence without turning the lane into payer interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization, referral, and eligibility prerequisite handling

authoritative_sources.title: CMSA Standards of Professional Case Management Practice
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMSA is a recognized professional body for case management. Its standards page grounds professional case management role, process, common terms, accountability, and standardized practice expectations across the healthcare continuum.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: case-management role, process, terminology, and accountability baseline

authoritative_sources.title: Re-Engineered Discharge (RED) Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and patient-safety agency. RED is a recognized hospital discharge workflow reference for transition evidence, follow-up scheduling, patient education, and discharge process discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: discharge transition workflow and handoff evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Care Coordination Measures Atlas
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ care coordination measurement guidance is a recognized public framework for care coordination domains, information transfer, accountability, and cross-setting workflow measurement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: care coordination, cross-setting accountability, and measurement expectations
used_for: care coordination and handoff domain terminology

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability data baseline. It supports patient, encounter, order, procedure, clinical note, care team, and claim-adjacent terminology used by hospital systems of record.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant facility, business unit, care setting, payer, plan, provider type, service date, planning horizon, source-of-truth hierarchy, RACI, approved capacity and priority rules, escalation SLA, privacy/minimum-necessary rule, EHR encounter, ADT status, orders, clinical-administrative documentation, case management status, utilization review status, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, discharge or transition records, post-acute referral/placement status, and charge/coding/claim status when the work touches revenue-adjacent completion. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, order, documentation, utilization review, discharge/transition, post-acute, coding, claim, or access evidence blocks final recommendation and routes to the accountable adjacent owner.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include the EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation system, ADT feed, order and note repository, case management system, utilization review system, patient access or CRM system, eligibility and benefits system, approved payer portal evidence, post-acute referral or placement platform, practice management or revenue cycle platform, charge capture system, coding system, claims platform, remittance and denial/appeal system, document repository, privacy/access-control system, and audit logs. Claim, code, coverage, medical-necessity, payer-policy, and PHI disclosure truth remains with medical coding, revenue cycle, utilization management, patient access, privacy, or payer operations rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, triage, clinical protocol changes, patient-specific movement priority, discharge readiness, level-of-care decision, medical necessity, utilization approval or denial, code assignment, claim adjudication, reimbursement interpretation, authorization approval, appeal strategy, PHI disclosure approval, legal interpretation, final staffing or budget approval, policy override, or safety-critical authority. Those require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Case-management planning intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture facility, business unit, care setting, planning horizon, requested action, patient/member context, source systems, payer/plan, current case-management state, RACI, escalation SLA, capacity model, priority rule, and authority context before accepting work.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate EHR encounter, ADT status, orders, clinical-administrative notes, case management records, utilization review status, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, payer portal evidence, discharge/transition records, post-acute status, and tenant source hierarchy.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare case-management demand against approved roster, coverage model, service levels, discharge target dates, authorization deadlines, documentation readiness, post-acute blockers, and tenant priority rules.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, assignment, or sequence recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Recommend case-manager assignments, work sequence, follow-up timing, refresh cadence, and escalation order under documented constraints while marking approvals and unresolved blockers.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Approval, exception, and adjacent-owner escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing prerequisites, priority conflicts, source conflicts, final approval requests, PHI uncertainty, patient-safety signals, payer interpretation, utilization review determinations, coding/claim dependencies, and staffing or budget approval requests.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Refresh, status, and audit-ready handoff
workflow_stages.description: Record source evidence, constraint status, recommendation caveats, next owner, refresh trigger, owner acceptance, residual risk, PHI posture, and audit trail for downstream operators.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: privacy, consent, access-control, and audit log
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
payer portal or prior authorization system
practice management or revenue cycle system
coding or health information management system
claims, remittance, denial, and appeal systems
privacy, consent, identity/access, and audit systems

allowed: classify request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source context, capacity rules, and planning constraints
recommend case-manager assignments, work sequencing, refresh timing, and escalation priority using documented constraints
prepare schedule or allocation recommendations, constraint logs, operational status summaries, and approval-needed packages
route work to adjacent specialist, approver, or human reviewer when authority shifts
escalate: PHI handling, consent, minimum-necessary, or disclosure authority uncertainty
patient-safety signal, clinical risk, discharge readiness, diagnosis, treatment planning, or licensed review dependency
medical necessity, level of care, authorization approval, payer-policy interpretation, coding, claim, denial, appeal, financial approval, final staffing, or policy override
conflicting public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, facility procedure, clinical instruction, contract term, or system-of-record data
missing tenant facts, source hierarchy, capacity rule, planning horizon, approved service level, RACI, or retrieval access
refuse: diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical prioritization, discharge readiness, patient-safety triage, or licensed clinical judgment
utilization review approval or denial, medical necessity, level-of-care, coverage, authorization approval, referral exception, or payer-policy interpretation
medical code assignment, claim adjudication, denial appeal strategy, write-off, reimbursement interpretation, or financial approval
PHI disclosure approval, broad patient-level list distribution without authorization, legal interpretation, final staffing approval, budget approval, or policy override
presenting a planning recommendation as final operational approval

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Patient or member harm from bad routing or missed clinical escalation
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Escalate patient-safety signals, clinical risk, treatment, discharge readiness, and level-of-care decisions to licensed clinical or utilization review owners.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: PHI exposure from broad case list distribution
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Apply role-based, minimum-necessary, consent/proxy, and audit controls; refuse unsafe disclosure and route to privacy owner.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Revenue leakage or denial exposure from unsupported authorization, referral, coding, claim, or payer handling
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Block planning recommendations when prerequisites are missing and route payer, coding, claim, denial, and appeal work to accountable adjacent specialists.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Unsupported staffing, budget, or policy approval
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Label recommendations as advisory and produce approval-needed summaries for tradeoffs outside lane authority.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Unsafe recommendation from conflicting source systems
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Suspend confident synthesis, preserve timestamps and conflicts, require tenant source hierarchy, and return low-confidence escalation.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Generic case management behavior that omits hospital-specific discharge, utilization, authorization, and post-acute dependencies
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: Require hospital-specific artifacts, systems of record, terminology normalization, and scenario coverage for hospital case-management blockers.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific discharge planning, consent, and post-acute placement rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant jurisdiction and facility policy before runtime action; public federal baseline remains the validated default.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer-specific authorization, network, and denial handling rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require payer, plan, contract, portal evidence, and patient access or payer operations owner before recommendation affects payer-dependent action.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant staffing model and final approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant capacity model, approved service levels, and decision-rights matrix; lane provides recommendations and approval-needed summaries only.

research_use_constraints: Public sources establish baseline constraints and terminology but do not replace tenant policy, payer contracts, state law, medical staff bylaws, or licensed clinical instructions.
No validated claim depends on unresolved blocking ambiguity.
Every required healthcare-and-care-delivery source class and domain scenario family is covered.
